extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series begins with a relatively high value (around 0.5 or above) and then drops steeply to a low point (around 0.2 or below) within the first ~20 time steps.  
R2: After this early drop, the series stays low for a while (a flat or gently varying segment near its minimum).  
R3: In the final third of the series, the values rise again to a medium or high level (around 0.4–0.7) with mild fluctuations.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series begins at a medium level (around 0.3–0.5) and then decreases gradually rather than in one steep plunge in the first ~20 time steps.  
R2: After reaching a minimum (around 0.1 or slightly above), the series increases to a stable plateau in the middle segment (roughly constant around 0.4–0.6).  
R3: In the last part of the series, the values often peak briefly above the plateau (a short sharp rise) but do not show the pronounced early high-then-deep-drop pattern of Class 0.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has a prominent early peak (around the first 5–10% of the time) that is among the highest points of the whole series.  
R2: After this early peak, the series drops down to a clear low valley (near the first 15–25% of the time).  
R3: In the second half of the series, the values stay roughly in a mid-range band without any new peak that is as high as the initial peak.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series also has an early peak, but later in the series (middle or last third) there is another peak that is similar to or higher than the early one.  
R2: The middle portion of the series (roughly 30–70% of the time) tends to stay at a moderate level, without a long flat low valley after the early peak.  
R3: The last third of the series contains a noticeable rise or spike, so the end region is not flatter/lower than the middle.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After an initial drop, the series reaches its minimum around the middle (roughly indices 30–40) and then clearly rises again afterwards.  
R2: In the last third, the series shows an upward or flat trend, often ending at a value noticeably above the mid-minimum (typically above 0.3).  
R3: There is no long, nearly flat plateau after the mid-minimum; instead, the post-minimum segment continues to move upward or fluctuate upward.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After an initial drop, the series reaches its minimum around the middle (roughly indices 30–40) and then rises only once.  
R2: After this rise, the series stays in a relatively flat plateau for the rest of the sequence, with only small fluctuations (no strong new uptrend).  
R3: The final values lie in a narrow band slightly above the mid-minimum (typically around 0.35–0.5), without a pronounced late increase.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After an initial spike and drop, the series reaches its global minimum roughly in the middle of the sequence (around indices 30–40).
R2: Following this mid-sequence minimum, the series shows a sustained upward trend.
R3: The final segment (last third) stays at a medium level (around 0.4–0.5) without a strong upward or downward trend.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After an initial spike and drop, the series reaches its global minimum earlier than mid-sequence (roughly before index 30).
R2: From this early minimum, the series rises to a medium level and forms a broad plateau (values roughly 0.4–0.55) that starts around the middle of the sequence.
R3: The final segment (last third) is flat or gently decreasing, and does not show a late strong upward trend.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After an initial peak in the first 10–15% of the series, the signal falls steadily to a clear minimum around 30–40% of the series length.  
R2: From that minimum, the signal rises again and stays mostly at a medium–high level (around 0.4–0.6) up to about 70–80% of the series.  
R3: In the last 20–30% of the series there is either a pronounced peak or at least maintained high values compared to the earlier minimum.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After an initial peak in the first 10–15% of the series, the signal drops steadily to a clear minimum around 25–35% of the series length.  
R2: From that minimum, the signal rises back toward a medium level but then remains roughly flat or gently declining, without forming a strong late peak.  
R3: The last 20–30% of the series stays at a medium or medium–low plateau (around 0.3–0.5), clearly lower and less variable than any early or mid-series peaks.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the first 10% of time, the series either gently decreases or stays around a mid value (no sharp spike above ~0.8 followed by a rapid drop).  
R2: Between 10% and 40% of time, the series remains relatively low and flat (values mostly between ~0.15 and 0.35).  
R3: In the last 20% of time, there is a clear rising trend culminating in a late high peak (values rising from ~0.4 up to at least ~0.7–0.8 near the end).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the first 10% of time, there is a sharp, early peak (value quickly rises above ~0.8) followed by a rapid drop.  
R2: Between 10% and 40% of time, the series continues to decline to a clear minimum (around ~0.15–0.25) before starting to rise again.  
R3: After this minimum, the series rises to a moderate plateau in the mid-range (~0.4–0.5) and then stays roughly flat or slowly declines toward the end without a strong late peak.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The initial peak (within the first 10% of the series) is moderate, not exceeding approximately 0.75.  
R2: After the initial drop, the lowest point of the series remains above approximately 0.10.  
R3: In the second half of the series (after the midpoint), values tend to stay at or above about 0.40 for most of the time.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The initial peak (within the first 10% of the series) is strong, typically reaching around or above 0.85.  
R2: After the initial drop, the series descends to a very low minimum, near or below approximately 0.05.  
R3: In the second half of the series (after the midpoint), values tend to stay mostly between about 0.30 and 0.45, without a sustained rise above 0.50.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After an initial peak and decline, the series has a long, relatively flat low region in the middle (around indices 20–45) with values mostly below 0.3.  
R2: Towards the end (after about index 55), the series shows a clear secondary rise to a high peak (around or above 0.7) before finishing.  
R3: The late high peak (R2) is noticeably higher than the plateau that immediately precedes it (a jump of at least ~0.2 in value).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial peak and decline, the series enters a long, smooth rise beginning from a low valley (around indices 25–40) up to a mid-level plateau (around 0.45–0.55).  
R2: In the last third (after about index 55), the series remains roughly flat or slightly decreasing around this mid-level; it does not exhibit a new high peak above ~0.6.  
R3: The middle and late parts look like one broad rise then gentle plateau, without a distinct late surge that clearly exceeds the earlier plateau.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the first major peak early in the series, the signal spends a long middle segment at a relatively low level (around 0.1–0.35) compared with its early peak.  
R2: In the late part of the series, there is either a clear rising trend or at least one pronounced late peak that brings the value back to a high level (around 0.5–0.8).  
R3: The final values of the series are among the highest values in the entire sequence (no strong drop at the very end).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the first major peak early in the series, the signal drops and then rises into a mid‑series plateau around a medium–high level (about 0.4–0.6) without a long low segment.  
R2: In the late part of the series, the signal does not show a strong sustained increase compared with the mid‑series level; it stays roughly similar or decreases.  
R3: The final values of the series are not the highest; they are comparable to or lower than the mid‑series plateau.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial peak and decline (around time 10–20), the series reaches its minimum later, near time 30–40, not very early.
R2: After this minimum, the series rises to a clear secondary hump roughly in the middle of the window (around time 40–60).
R3: In the last third of the window (after about time 60), the series stays relatively flat or slowly varying, without a strong upward or downward trend.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial peak and decline, the series continues to decrease so that its global minimum occurs quite early, before about time 30.
R2: From that early minimum, the series shows a long, sustained rise through the middle of the window (roughly time 30–60).
R3: In the final third of the window (after about time 60), the series is at or near a local maximum and is generally higher than its early minimum, often slightly declining or flattening.
